title: T-022 — API: Routings + routing_operations CRUD
prompt:
# T-022 — API: Routings + routing_operations CRUD

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §12.1, §12.2, §12.4, §12.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Routings CRUD with sequence + resource validation; approve/publish workflow.

Use the same supersede pattern as BOMs. Cost preview computation lives separately (T-023). manufacturing_operation_name link to bom_lines field used for cross-checks but no DB FK.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/routings/route.ts [create]
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/routings/[version]/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/routing/service.ts [create]
- tests/api/routings.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cost preview (T-023)
- Resource utilization Gantt computation (Phase 2 detail)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given operations [1,2,4], when POST runs, then 422 V-TEC-60 (gap).
2. Given op without line_id and machine_id, when POST, then 422 V-TEC-61.
3. Given run_time_per_unit_sec=0 for production op, when POST, then 422 V-TEC-62.
4. Given manufacturing_operation_name not in reference_table, when POST, then 422 V-TEC-63.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for each V-TEC-60..63 violation.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/routings.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not delete routing rows (soft supersede only).
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
